questions.text: Why should a magnesium ribbon be cleaned before burning in air?
options: To remove the protective layer of magnesium oxide
questions.explanation: Magnesium reacts with oxygen in the air to form a layer of magnesium oxide which prevents further burning; cleaning removes this layer.

questions.text: A reaction in which a single product is formed from two or more reactants is known as a:
questions.explanation: In a combination reaction, multiple reactants join to form one single product, like the burning of coal.

questions.text: What happens when dilute hydrochloric acid is added to iron filings?
options: Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced
Chlorine gas and iron hydroxide are produced
Iron salt and water are produced
questions.explanation: Fe + 2HCl -> FeCl2 + H2. This is a displacement reaction where iron displaces hydrogen.

questions.text: The addition of oxygen to a substance or the removal of hydrogen from a substance is called:
questions.explanation: Oxidation involves the gain of oxygen or loss of hydrogen.

questions.text: The process of black coating on silver and green coating on copper are examples of:
questions.explanation: Corrosion occurs when a metal is attacked by substances around it such as moisture and acids.
